subcodes.title: Address at Turanian Society of Budapest, Budapest, 1913-04-14
subcodes.first_line: What a source of pride this is for humanity—that in Budapest, which is in the West, a society should be formed for the advancement and improvement of the condition of the Easterners...Qurratu'l-'Ayn (Tahirih) was eloquent. Her writings and poems are available today. Every one of the learned of the region praised her... In the Christian Dispensation it was Mary Magdalene who was the cause of the steadfastness of the disciples
subcodes.first_line_orig: در عالم انسانی این چه قدر مدار افتخار است که در بوداپست مملکت غرب انجمنی تشکیل شود
subcodes.subjects: @Tahirih [Qurratu'l-'Ayn], @role and station of women, @equality of men and women, @prejudice; racial prejudice; class distinction, @harmony of science and religion, @oneness; unity of religion, @unity; oneness of humanity
